Harry Potter and Harry Potter

dark dhampir [archived by HarryPotterFanFicArchive_Archivist]

Summary:

The Battle of the Department of Mysteries goes a little differently when Harry gets some help from an alternate reality's verison of himself. The alternate Harry has come to save his others self from a dark wizard from his own reality, but he has other news. Ron and Dumbledore are Harry's enemies, and . . . Harry has twelve wives?! Crossover with the DArk Tower Saga and the Stargate franchise. Warning: has femslash.

Notes:

Note from the archivist: This story was originally archived at the Harry Potter Fanfic Archive and was moved to the AO3 as part of the Open Doors project in 2022. We tried to reach out to all creators about the move and posted announcements, but may not have reached everyone. If you are the creator and would like to claim this work, please contact us using the e-mail address on the Harry Potter Fanfic Archive’s collection profile.

Disclaimer:  I do not own Harry Potter, The Dark Tower Saga, or the Stargate franchise.  If I did, they would all be very different.

 

IMPORTANT WARNING: This story contains swearing, violence, character death, potential drug use (don't know for sure yet), graphic sex (male/female, female/female, male/female-and-female/female-simultaniously, bondage, and S/M), complicated ideas, and examples of pure evil. There won't be much of any of this for the first few chapter's, however. If any of this offends you, stop reading now, but, if you're still interested in the story, I am publishing a more filtered version on Fanfiction.net.

 

This story is inspired by numerous Harry/harem stories, particularly Radaslab's The Harem War on Fanfiction.net, which was itself based off the slave/harem challenge set by Ranger Dragan, although I went for a soul-bond theme instead of a slave story. My hat's off to RD for coming up with such a challenge and even more so for Radaslab for writing such an awesome story. His concept of a coven and magical bonds reminded me of Stephen King's concept of a ka-tet, and everything just took off from there. If anyone wants the rules to Ranger Dragan's challenge, either read the first chapter of Radaslab's THW, or go to the author's notes at the end of chapeter 0.

 

This takes place near the end of Order of the Phoenix.  IMPORTANT!!!  All World is different in my story from the one/ones in Stephen King’s stories.  Instead of post-apocalyptic American West, my All World (or what we see of it, at least) is a steampunk-styled world.  I just borrowed a lot of Mr. King’s ideas and a few from Stargate.

Chapter 1: Chapter 0: The Door and the Steed

Chapter Text


Author's notes:

On his way to save his godfather, Harry encounters the gunslinger.

PROLOUGE: THE DOOR AND THE STEED

ALL WORLD:

The door was made of ghostwood and had four words written on it in the Great Letter:, THE BOY WHO LIVED. The knocker and the knob were both gold, or were at least both gold-plated. The hinges were ordinary.

The Boy-Who-Lived . . . of course, the gunslinger thought. I should have suspected as much. But, suspicion had never been a quality the gunslinger had ever possessed great amounts of, except for those whom he actively disliked.

One of his companions smiled. "It is rather ironically appropriate." She knew of his chagrin, such was the bond they shared, the bond that was ka-an-tet.

Her words comforted him somewhat. That was good, because what he did next would not be easy. When he went through that door, he could very well find himself in a place where people walked upon the clouds and drank molten stone instead of water. Worse, he would be alone. That was much his own decision, however, for he would not risk his loved ones so easily.

The gunslinger turned back to his most fervent allies. For a brief moment, he considered saying farewell, but he quickly crushed the impulse. That was not what they needed to hear. "I shall return," he said, with fierce determination. Then, he stretched out his hand to the knob.

The gunslinger opened the door and went through.

EN ROUTE FROM HOGWARTS TO THE MINISTRY OF MAGIC:

Harry Potter was not happy as he flew to England on the back of a thresal, a skeletal-looking winged horse which only those who had seen death could see. Unfortunately, Harry had witnessed the murder of Cedric Diggory the year before, so he could see the creatures. Despite their demonic appearance, however, they were benevolent and highly intelligent creatures who wouldn't hurt anyone unprovoked.

The reason Harry was riding one of these creatures was because he was flying to the Ministry of Magic to rescue his godfather, Sirius Black who was currently at the mercy of Harry's archenemy, Lord Voldemort. Voldemort, however, was merciless, and he was undoubtedly torturing Sirius at that very moment. Every second counted.

It may have been that because he was so concerned over Sirius that Harry did not immediately notice that his was no longer the only consciousness in his brain. He did notice when the voice spoke within his thoughts.

I did it; I've crossed over . . . into his mind. This caught Harry's attention but not as much as when the voice shouted He knows I'm here!

The gunslinger retreated deep into Harry's mind, almost leaving completely. That had been close. It would not do for Harry to be aware of his existence just yet, especially while the boy was flying on the back of a thresal.

The gunslinger steadied himself for a moment, then began to go into the mind of the Boy-Who-Lived, not forward, but deeper. The fact that the boy was riding a thresal was suggestive, and he was anxious to discover if it meant what he thought it meant. It did not take long to find the information he sought.

The gunslinger knew that time moved differently in different worlds, and, besides that, ghostwood doors could easily transcend times, as they transcended spaces. Thus, although it was the year two thousand and nineteen in All World, it was only the year of the one thousand, nine hundred and ninety-five in this world, but that was not what mattered.

What mattered was the event. The gunslinger knew what was happening. Voldemort had discovered Harry's ability to see into his mind and had constructed a trap for the boy. The vision of Sirius was a lie. Voldemort wanted Harry to go the Ministry to find a prophecy about the two of them, a prophecy which might explain why Harry was such a danger to the Dark Lord. Ironically, Sirius was killed trying to rescue Harry from Voldemort's servants, the vile ka-tet known as the Death Eaters. And it was none other than Bellatrix Lestrange, Sirius' own deranged cousin, who had cast the killing curse. The thought of Sirius Black's death brought up an even more terrible memory in the gunslinger's mind.

Now, it seemed, the gunslinger had a chance to stop this sad chain of events from occurring, at least in this world. The chances of it happening were mind-boggling, but the gunslinger did not to question them. Ka was a wheel whose one purpose was turn, and, in the end, it always brought a man back to the place from which he started.

But the gunslinger could not act now. When the time came, he would have one chance and one chance only. There were many unknowns in this situation. He could tell from a glance that the world he was in was very different from his own. Aside from that, there were other things, and other people, to consider. Changing this drama's outcome was not why he had come to this world. He could not afford to forget that.

So, the gunslinger waited, and he watched.

The Rules to Ranger Dragan's Slave/Harem Challenge (Warning, this is spoilers to the story):

1. Harry must accept/take on multiple slaves

a. These must all be female slaves

b. There must be two or more girls from each House at Hogwarts in the harem.

c. Harry may be brutal to his girls, but he does not neccessarily have to be.

2. This must be all Sirius' fault.

3. Dumbledore's a bad guy.

a. Dumbledore has been cheating Harry out of his inheritance and keeping secrets from him.

b. The old man is evil.

4. The Weasley's nust be based.

a. Not all of the Weasleys need to be evil or stupid, but at least some should be.

b. It is almost a Harry/multi requirement that Ron be one of he bashed.

5. Harry has his own island.

a. He inherited this island.

b. This island is located somewhere in the Atlantic, presumeably to be close to Britain.

That's all of it. I got to say, when you consider this, the books get a whole new outlook, and some things make more sense (Ex: Why didn't Dumbledore realize that his old friend Moody was Barty Crouch Jr. in disguise? Either the old man is evil, or he's stupid). Also, I agree with Radaslab on something. Harry can kick the bad guy's butts, but he shouldn't mess with his allies. A gray/dark Harry is all well and good, but he's not a sicko like Voldemort. BE WARNED, however, this does not mean that some of Harry's girls won't be into rough sex or bondage. That's not brutality, that's personal taste.

One last thing. It's Harry's twinner in All World that feels the full force of these rules. The Harry we all know and love doesn't get it that bad because some things are different in his world and because the gunslinger and company step in. If I get enough incetive or inspiration, however, I will write a prelude that will chronicle All World's Harry and his adventures. That story's much less developed, hwoever, so no promises.

Chapter 2: Chapter 1: Trap and DoubleTrap

Chapter Text


Author's notes:

Harry and his friends arrive at the Ministry, and Harry talks to someone in his head.

1. TRAP AND DOUBLE TRAP

THE MINISTRY OF MAGIC:

Harry entered the Ministry with five of his friends: Ron Weasly, Hermione Granger, Ron's little sister Ginny, Neville Longbottom, and Luna Lovegood. He had not wanted them to come with him, but they had insisted. Harry did not like putting his friends in danger, but he knew he would need all the help he could get.

They began to search the Ministry for Sirius, but there was no sign of him. Harry was getting more and more worried. Sirius could be killed, or tortured into a coma like Neville's parent's had been, or Merlin knew what. Harry could not stand that thought. Sirius had been his father's best friend and was more or less the only paternal figure in Harry's life. The young wizard simply could not lose him.

Then, there was that voice. He was used to hearing voices that other people did not. In most people, this was a sign of insanity. In Harry Potter, it was a sign of how messed up his whole life was. In his second year, he had heard the voice of a basilisk, an enormous snake monster, hidden in the plumbing. Ever since last summer, he had begun having terrible dreams that seemed to be inspired by Voldemort.

This voice was different. It did not have the serpentine quality of Parsletongue, the language of snakes, and it was certainly not Voldemort's voice. The voice had awe and fear in it, and these were things Voldemort never showed to anyone, if he even felt them. Comparatively, this voice was much more pleasantly human.

Harry had not told the others about it, because he had more pressing concerns. Later, perhaps, he would bring it up. Now was not the time.

The gunslinger, meanwhile, was looking out for his own concerns. He made one quick trip back to his own world to plot with his companions. When he returned, he prepared himself for what he knew had to come next. He would make contact with the Boy-Who-Lived.

Hile, Harry Potter.

Harry, upon hearing the voice, turned to Neville to ask what the strange phrase meant. Then, the voice shouted.

Don't look at him! I'm here, in your mind!

Harry froze in his place.

"Harry," Hermione asked. "What's wrong? Why are did you stop?" The others turned to look at him with concern.

This is it, Harry thought. You want to know why I've stopped, Hermione; I'll tell you. I've officially lost my mind. He opened his mouth. . .

Then, immediately re-shut it as the voice began shouting again. No, you fool, no! I am not a sign of madness. Our spirits share your mind for the moment.

Who are you? Harry mentally questioned the presence in his mind.

The young wizard heard the voice cry He believes me! Thank God, he believes me!

Listen, Harry Potter, do not tell your friends of me. You couldn't make them believe in me, and we don't have the time. Just say you thought you saw something and start walking again. Hurry!

Who are you? Harry insisted.

I'm your friend. You need know nothing more of me for the time being. Now, do as I say and pay attention to what I tell you.

"I thought I saw something," Harry said, deliberately avoiding eye contact with his friends. "It was nothing."

"Okay," Ron said, not totally convinced.

"Are you sure, Harry?" Hermione asked, her voice laced with concern.

"I am," Harry lied, hating the deception. He started walking. "C'mon, let's get moving. Sirius needs us."

The others followed, though not as comfortable as they were before, which was saying something. Remember that they were a half-dozen underage wizards and witches illegally waltzing around the most important building in magical Britain. They were doing this to rescue a grown and fully trained wizard from an unknown number of the foulest sorcerers alive.

Hermione was particularly worried. She had seen something. She wasn't sure if any of the others had; Ron probably had not. Harry may not have even noticed it himself, but his head had bent to one side while he was considering how to answer her, almost as if he had been listening to something.

And didn't it take him a while to decide that there wasn't anything to see? She thought so. There was something, she did not know what, Harry was not telling them. It hurt and frightened her. But Harry was right. They need to concentrate on Sirius, for now. She would confront her friend later. That did not mean, however, that she would not keep a half eye on him for the moment.

I hope you're happy, Harry told the voice. I've lied to my friends, satisfied?

It's not the first time you have done so, the voice answered, calmly. For a moment, Harry wanted to rave at the git in his mind, but then the voice added. But do not believe that I think nothing of this deception. It is necessary, but I cry your pardon, wizard.

Harry did not know how to react. The voice just kept getting stranger and stranger. "I cry your pardon?" Stranger still, the voice sounded genuinely remorseful. Could he trust it? Harry decided to reserve judgment for the moment. What are you so desperate to talk about?

You are all in danger. I have a plan, but will not be easy.

Harry resisted the urge to roll his eyes. Of course, it won't. Now tell me what this danger is and what your plan is.

I will, but, I warn you, you will not like it.

The voice told him. He did not like it.

The gunslinger knew that Harry would dislike the plan. He did not like it himself; the plan was incredibly risky. It was not that their stratagem (if one was generous enough to call it such a fine name) was a roughly planned affair, for the gunslinger (and a number of his companions, come to that) had always been gifted with improvisation. There were those who said the gunslinger acted best when he thought least. No, the plan was risky because it relied heavily on chance. Both the gunslinger and Harry had relied on ka to come to their aid in the past, which was an invitation to bad luck.

It could not be helped, however. The Hall of Prophecy drew nigh and with it came the moment to act. As soon as the sneaking group was safely within the room of the glowing spheres, the gunslinger spoke a single word: Now!

Harry stopped abruptly. "Get your wands ready," he whispered. Then, he spun around and pointed his own toward the shadows behind him. "Show yourselves!"

Immediately a group of Death Eaters appeared: Lucius Malfoy, Bellatrix Lestrange, and ten others. Malfoy was in front, signifying that he was in charge of the group. He did not look particularly happy.

"Well done, Potter. I suppose we shouldn't have expected anything less from the Boy-Who-Lived."

"Hello, there, little Nevilles," Bellatrix trilled. "How are Mummy and Daddy doing?"

Neville glowered at her. "They're doing well," he answered, his voice stern. Harry understood his anger. Bellatrix and some of her Death Eater friends had tortured Neville's parents into a coma fourteen years ago.

"Now, now, Bella," Lucius interrupted, mock compassion in his voice. "We aren't here to discuss the past. We're here to discuss the future."

"You want a prophecy," Harry said. It was not a question.

Malfoy smiled a little. "Clever, boy, very clever. Yes, we do want a prophecy, and you're going to get it for us."

"Harry's not going to help you!" Ginny declared. She aimed her wand at Malfoy. Suddenly, there was a burst of magic, and Harry and all his friends were wandless.

"Oh yes, Harry is going to help us," Malfoy said, his smile widening. "Unless, of course, he wants to watch his friends die one by one. Who do you think should the first, Bella? Weasley's little princess or the mudblood?"

"All right!" Harry growled. "I'll get your prophecy."

Malfoy was beaming now. "Wise move, Potter." Harry looked at that smile and wanted to punch it with all his might. To his pleasant surprise, the other in his mind wanted to do the same.

Maybe we can be friends after all, Harry thought. He turned and began to walk forward into the Hall.

Hermione grabbed him by the arm. "Harry, you can't! If you help them-"

Harry cut her off. "I don't have a choice, Hermione."

"He's right," Malfoy stated arrogantly. "He doesn't."

Hermione's eyes flicked from Harry, to Malfoy, and back again. Her hand tightened around his arm while her mind groped for an idea, any idea. Then, she felt Luna touch her free hand. Her eyes moved to the other girl.

The blond gave a small smile and winked. "Don't worry," that look said, "it'll be all right."

Hermione sighed and let go of Harry. Who would have ever thought she'd listen to Looney Lovegood's advice?

Although she did not know it, Harry had seen the look that passed between the two girls. For some reason he did not understand, it comforted him, pleased him even. The gunslinger saw, too, but, unlike Harry, he understood his joy. He understood Harry's, too. It was all a part of ka-an-tet.

"Wait just a minute, Potter," Malfoy said as Harry began again to walk into Hall. "Maybe one or two of us should go with you. We wouldn't want you to get lost, now would we?"

He thinks I'm going to try to run away, Harry angrily thought, as two wizards who were probably Crabbe and Goyle's fathers started to follow him. He thinks I'm cowardly enough to leave my friends behind.

I wont, the voice said, which Harry took to mean, "I suppose so". Cowards often assume cowardice in others. Or, mayhap he thinks you'll try to smash the prophecy. Mayhap there's even a small part of him that suspects you could lay a trap for him and his friends.

Harry smiled grimly. He should pay more attention to that part.

The gunslinger's trap was about to spring.

Chapter 3: Chapter 2: Duel and Shootout

Chapter Text

2. DUEL AND SHOOTOUT

Harry led Crabbe and Goyle through the Hall to the prophecy that was about him and Voldemort. Harry, of course, had the other to guide him. It was, he supposed, good that Malfoy had chosen to send the goons with him. Another pair might have been suspicious of how he knew exactly where to go.

When the time comes, the voice warned him, we'll have to move fast or we'll all find ourselves in hot oust. Harry guessed this meant that, if they didn't move like lightening, their goose was cooked.

Obviously, the wizard thought.

The pair made their move as soon as Crabbe and Goyle followed him to the shelf that held the prophecy. The gunslinger came forward to the front of Harry's mind. Though it frightened Harry, he allowed it to happen. It was the only chance he had to save his friends.

Crabbe and Goyle could barely cast spells with their wands and voices. Wandless and silent spells were impossible for them. Needless to say, therefore, they were quite surprised when a fifth year turned and disarmed them with nothing more than a wave of his hand. Before they had time to figure out how to react to this new development, the same fifth year hit them both with a wandless, silent spell that knocked them both out. The gunslinger then grabbed their bodies and laid them silently on the floor. It was hard to accomplish with Harry's scrawny body, but necessary. No need for Malfoy and company to get a heads up on what was coming their way.

The gunslinger would have rather not used magic on these two, but he was not sure how good Harry's body would be in a brawl. Actually, he was not sure Harry's untrained body could do the spells without the aid of a wand and spoken words. If it had not, then he and the young wizard would have needed to move fast to even the playing field. Speaking of which . . .

You know what you have to do, he said as he retreated into Harry's mind.

I do, Harry affirmed. He saw it, right behind Crabbe and Goyle, a doorway to another world. It had followed him from behind the Death Eaters to its current position. There were people on the other side, gathered around the body of a man.

It's your body, isn't it? he asked the other, although he already knew. Then, knowing that he was entrusting his friends' lives, as well as his own, to a group of complete strangers, Harry walked over the slumbering Crabbe and Goyle and into the world of the gunslinger.

Harry had been expecting to feel some sort of sensation when he crossed worlds, but there was none. There was neither a tug at his navel like when he used portkeys, nor the feeling of being squeezed through a rubber tube he had been told Apparition caused. He did not even feel dizzy. He just walked from one world into another.

ALL WORLD:

The young wizard got his first real shock when he actually crossed worlds. It was not the place, which looked like a big field. It was the people. Except for the man who was rising to his feet, the other, all of them were women. But it was not that discovery, nor their strange clothes that bothered Harry.

"You, you're all . . ." It was too bizarre a concept to be put to words.

"We are, indeed," one of the women, a blonde, said, "and yet we are not. But that's not important right now."

"Right," the other said. "Saving your friends is what's important." It was then, looking at the other full in the face, that Harry got an even bigger shock, and yet, it seemed like something he should have expected.

He looked at Harry for a moment, then, smiled grimly. "Are you ready?"

Swallowing his bewilderment, Harry answered calmly. "I don't have a wand."

"Barrow mine, then," the other said, drawing a wand out of a holster on his right hip.

As Harry took the wand, the women looked concerned. "Are you sure about that?" one asked, her voice held a slight French accent.

The other turned to her and patted the weapon on his left hip. "I'll be able to handle myself."

"How do we do this?" Harry asked.

A brunette answered. "You two will grab each other, then we'll all grab onto you or each other. "I'm not completely sure if this will work, but it should get at least the two of you through."

Accordingly, Harry and the other grabbed each other's elbows. This accomplished, half the women grabbed him, and the rest grabbed the other. Before they began walking, however, Harry had to ask one last question. "You promise that you'll explain all this to me later, right?"

The other nodded. "That was our agreement. We'll hold palaver when the time comes. It is necessary."

Then, in an awkward motion, that involved half the group walking backwards. They all crossed back into Harry's world.

THE MINISTRY OF MAGIC:

Lucius Malfoy was having a wonderful evening. The Dark Lord would be so pleased with him when he delivered Potter and the prophecy to him . . . and with the five corpses of Potter's friends, to boot. His past mistake with the diary, a mistake he still did not grasp the full scope of, would be forgiven and forgotten. The only thing that bothered him was the fact that the boy had seen through his and the others' Disillusionment charms. No matter, there would be time enough for questions later. In the meantime, he was enjoying the looks on the other children's faces.

The group in question mostly bore looks of grim determination. Hermione and Neville were both horrified that Harry would actually do what the Death Eaters wanted. Ginny secretly hoped Harry would come up with a plan to save them all. Luna, by contrast, believed that Harry already had a plan and was only waiting for the chance to spring into action. Of them all, only Ron was truly calm. This was because he had secretly contacted the Order of the Phoenix before they had even left the school. Any moment now, they would show up and save all their lives. As such, he was struggling not to look smug, which only succeeded in making him look constipated.

Malfoy, of course, understood exactly what the girls and the Longbottom boy were thinking. The Weasley boy, he supposed, was the only one who understood exactly how doomed they all were. Imagine that, he thought, an intelligent Weasley. He would enjoy explaining the situation to the other four. He would enjoy it immensely.

It was at that point that the situation began to change drastically, where (to Malfoy) it began to go horribly wrong. Powerful spells flew from his right, disarming all ten Death Eaters and knocking them down. It took Malfoy a moment to get untangled from the others and get his bearings again. He looked to see who had ambushed them. He did not like what he saw.

Although he did not know it, the children has seen the as well, and were now wondering who had fired them. Ron, of course, had thought it was the Order. The others assumed it had been Ministry security. The newcomers were obviously neither.

Harry was one of them, holding a new wand in his hand (his own still, formerly in Malfoys possession, now lay on the floor with the others), yet it looked just like his old one. Beside him stood twelve women, all of whom were pointing wands at the Death Eaters. Some were even pointing revolvers at them, even though every wizard in the known world looked down on firearms (assuming they even knew what such things were). All were wearing jeans, long dusters, vests over their plain shirts, and wide brimmed hats that veiled their eyes in shadow. Belts of bullets crossed the hips of the ones holding guns. Hermione, due to her Muggle background was the only one who had anyone to compare these people to. They looked like desperados from the American Old West, gunslingers.

Aside from Harry, one other man stood among the women. He was taller than Harry, with equally dark hair. He wore gun belts, but held nothing in his hands, yet his stance in the group, front and center, suggested he was their leader. Harry stood beside him.

"Hile, Malfoy," he said evenly. Then, there was a streak of blue lightning by his left hip and the crash of thunder, and one of the Death Eaters was clutching his arm in pain, the wand he had silently called to his hand broken in two.

"Next person to try something will lose more than just a wand, understand?" the man asked. His left hand now held a smoking gun, larger than the ones the women carried. He had drawn and fired it faster than the eye could follow.

Harry left the man's side to go to his friends. "Are you all okay?" he asked.

"Yes," Hermione answered, looking at the others who nodded in agreement. "We're all all right."

"Harry," Ron asked, "who the bloody hell are they?" The red haired wizard was annoyed that it had not been the Order which had come to save them.

Harry looked back at the mysterious group for a moment, before answering. "They're friends, I think."

"Very good friends, apparently," Luna noted with approval. Ron just grunted.

Lucius struggled from his rear to his knees. "Stay where you are, Malfoy," the man warned, thumbing back the gun's hammer, "unless you want me to put some holes in you. I would be very happy to do so, if that is your wish." There was a cold edge in his voice. No one who heard it could believe the wandless gunman was lying.

The six students looked at each other. Where these people really friends, or just more psychopaths?

One of the women spoke, a blonde. "He's got something in his hand. Hand it over, Malfoy." Her wand glowed menacingly. Next to the cocked gun, it was an intimidating sight.

Malfoy glowered at her. "Of course, my dear." Then, he threw the object at the gunslingers.

"Move!" the mysterious man cried. Instantly, the unknown team ran toward the six wizards, some firing off hexes or bullets as they did so. They acted as one, with synchronization in their movement that seemed magical. The Death Eaters, meanwhile scrambled back with much less unity. A few even managed to rearm themselves in the confusion.

The object (a small, glass orb filled with white smoke in it, like a Remembrall) exploded on contact with the floor, releasing an enormous cloud of smoke.

The gunslingers herded the wizards behind the shelves and took up defensive positions. Not a moment too soon, as curses began exploding through the smokescreen. All the spells were aimed at their current position, so the Death Eaters must have had some means of locating them without dispelling the smoke.

The gunslingers knew what they were doing, however. The women easily parried and blocked the curses, then fired back with their wands and guns. The man, lacking a wand, was fanning his right hand over the back of his revolver. While the gunslingers couldn't see where their enemies were hiding, they aimed for where the attacks came from. Judging by the yelps and screams coming from the other side of the smokescreen, the tactic appeared to be working.

Harry heard footsteps behind him and a shrill laugh. Without thinking, he pivoted and shouted "Expelliarmus!"

Bellatrix Lestrange was knocked back; the wand she had picked up flew from her hand and snapped against the wall. Before the insane witch could do anything more than stare at her empty hand, Neville charged. He no longer had a wand, but he still had his fists. The Longbottom scion punched her hard in the face. This caused the back of her head to hit the wall. Bellatrix sank to the floor, moaning with a bleeding, and probably broken, nose.

After perhaps five or ten minutes, the curses stopped firing, and the smoke began to clear. When it was gone, there was no sign of the remaining dark wizards at all. Some of the gunslingers advanced and examined the place the Death Eaters had positioned themselves. There was no sign of them.

"Nothing," one of the women growled, holstering her gun in frustration.

"They must have fled when they realized that they were outmatched," another suggested.

"Cowards," a red-head growled.

The man nodded in agreement. "That they are; that they are." He walked back to Harry and the other students. Upon reaching Harry, he extended his right hand. "May I have my wand back, Harry Potter?"

For a moment, Harry considered holding onto it and demanding the other answer his questions. Then he remembered how fast the gunslinger had drawn his weapon, frighteningly fast. Harry decided to give the man his wand back.

"Thankee-sai," the man said, slipping it back into a specially designed holster on his right hip.

"Who are you?" Hermione asked, suddenly. "How did you get here? Why did you help us?"

The gunslingers looked at each other, evidently unsure how to answer the question. One of the blondes, however, smiled at them. "We are. . ." she started, but, just then, the doors burst open and people rushed into the Hall. The gunslingers automatically resumed their defensive stances.

Harry and the others returned to their place behind the shelves. When they peaked out, however, they realized they had nothing to fear. Even the gunslingers stood down upon realizing who the new intruders were.

It was some of the Order's best and most dangerous. Alistor "Mad-Eye" Moody was there, along Kingsly Shacklebolt, Nymphadora Tonks, Remus Lupin, and-

"Sirius!" Harry shouted, relieved to see his godfather alive.

"Harry," the older wizard answered, "are you all right? What happened here?"

"And who are these guys?" Moody asked, aiming his wand at the gunslingers.

"We are . . . friends," the other answered. Harry looked at him. All of the strange, otherworldly warriors had been visibly affected by the arrival of the Order, but the man seemed to have been the most. He seemed almost on the verge of tears.

The older members of the Order looked shocked, too, Lupin and Sirius in particular. "Who are you?" Sirius asked. "Why do you sound so much like James Potter?"

The gunslinger smiled at the Marauder and reached for his hat. The women followed his example. "I'm a friend. I want to help you all, and I think you might be able to help me and my own, too." Then, he and his companions removed their hats.

"I am Harry James Potter, son of James and Lily."

Chapter 4: Chapter 3: Secrets and Deceptions

Chapter Text


Author's notes:

 

SECRETS AND DECEPTIONS

THE MINISTRY OF MAGIC:

To say that every wizard (other than fifteen-year-old Harry Potter) in the Hall of Prophecy was shocked was an understatement. Wizards are accustomed to seeing things that are unexplainable by conventional science. Still, seeing older versions of people you knew (or yourself, in some cases) was bizarre. Still, that did not change what was there. The women gunslingers appeared to be older versions of none other than Hermione, Luna, Ginny, and Tonks, as well as Daphne and Astoria Greengrass form Slytherin, Padma and Pavarti Patel, Katie Bell from sixth year of Gryffindor, Susan Bones from Hufflepuff, Gabrielle Delacourte of France (she was too young, compared to the others, to be Fleur), and Narcissa Malfoy. Most amazing, the man who called himself Harry Potter had the real Harry's eyes and lightning bolt scar.

Perhaps "shocked" is not the proper word. Luna Lovegood was more surprised than shocked. In fact, she was pleasantly surprised. "Others?" she asked with a smile on her face.

Her doppelganger turned to her. "If you mean otherworldly versions of a person, then yes, although we call such beings twinners."

"Otherworldly what?" Hermione exclaimed.

Her twinner smiled at her. "We are versions of you and your friends who inhabit an alternate version of the reality in which you live."

"Huh?" Tonks, like a number of other individuals in the room, looked confused.

"Daphne" shook her head. "Sorry about that. We've more or less given up on her." The gunslinger Hermione scowled at the woman in mock fury, to which she smiled back. "Do you all know what happens when two mirrors face each other?"

"They create an infinite reflection," Lupin answered.

"Correct. Reality is fundamentally like those reflections. All of you, this whole planet, and every star in the night sky, are all inhabitants of a single reflection. We come from another. The two reflections are similar, but each is unique."

"Hench, the guns and the outfits." Ginny concluded. The Luna beside her was, if anything, smiling even more widely.

"I've always wanted to meet someone from another world. Everyone in my family has. My Mum was researching ways to enter them. That was how. . ." She broke off, her expression becoming more poignant. Harry understood. Luna's mother had died in an experiment years ago. It had probably being an experiment in crossing worlds.

Luna spoke again. "I wish she could be here to see this."

"That's nice," Moody said, gruffly (as he said pretty much everything), "But why the hell are you lot here, and with her?" He pointed at Narcissa to make his point clear.

The woman herself cast her eyes down in shame. The one next her, who looked like Susan Bones, put a hand on her shoulder. The others glowered at Moody. The older Harry looked particularly angry.

"She is one of us," he growled, "and she helped to save your young friends." He pointed at the young wizards in question. "Be grateful, fool, or hold your peace."

Moody did not look particularly remorseful. "Sorry, but around here she's a Death Eater, or at least the wife of a particularly nasty one."

At this point, Narcissa looked up. "If my twinner's relationship with Lucius Malfoy is at all like my own was, then it is no marriage."

"It doesn't matter anyway," Astoria's "twinner" said. "Our Cissy annulled her relationship with the Malfoy of our world years ago. And, she has no support for your world's Malfoy."

The Katie look-alike kicked the corner of one of the stacks. "I still wish we could have caught the arrogant pain in the ass."

Daphne nodded to her. "And I wish I had shot him or hexed him before he could have thrown that ball."

Harry then recognized the two from earlier. Daphne was the one who had seen Malfoy draw the smoke dispensing orb from his pocket, and Katie was the one who had first expressed displeasure when the Death Eaters had escaped.

His own twinner's scowl deepened. "He'll be dealt with in time. Just wait."

"Eh-hem," the otherworldly Hermione coughed. "Mayhap it would be best if we took this discussion to another place. I don't think we will remain alone much longer."

The young Hermione gasped. "She's right! The Ministry's security guards will be here any minute."

Lupin nodded in agreement. "And, it would definitely be to our benefit not have to explain the presence of Sirius Black, six underage wizards, and thirteen otherworlders."

Kingley Shacklebolt spoke up. "I should go home, so I can be contacted by security."

"All right, Kingsley," Lupin said. "Go, then."

"Take care of yourselves," the dark skinned wizard replied. Then, before Disapparating away, he looked at the gunslingers. "Thank you," he said and then left.

"Where should we go?" Sirius asked.

"Not Headquarters," Moody growled. "I still don't trust them."

"Agreed," replied Lupin. Tonks looked reluctant, but nodded.

"What about our world?" the Gunslinger Harry asked. "The door's still open."

"Can we really trust you that much?" Tonks asked.

Her twinner grinned. "Can you trust yourself?"

Everyone paused to consider the implications. On one hand, another world was probably the most effective hiding place. Of course, that would put the wizards at the mercy of the gunslingers, who were still largely strangers at this point. However, the otherworldly wizard-warriors had fought in their defense and had put their weapons down when the members of the Order had entered. It was Harry, naturally, who ultimately made the decision.

"I trust you."

The others stared at him for a moment. Then Sirius laughed out loud. "That's good enough for me. Let's go."

The others agreed, after some discussion, to cross over. Moody finally gave in, but demanded that they "leave the door open." That settled, the twenty-three people formed a long line with a Harry Potter on either end. They looked for all the world like they were going to do a line dance. Then, they all turned around and walked back through the door.

ALL WORLD:

The group successfully traveled to the world of the gunslingers. The natives relaxed somewhat, happy to be back in a familiar place. Their guests, so to speak, looked around themselves in wonder. They were standing in a large, open field. Colorful flowers grew in various places, while a stream ran from their left to the distant space in front of them. Snowcapped mountains grew in the distance. To their right, there was a forest. The wizards took in the alien landscape.

"So this is another world," Sirius mused.

Mood growled. "I was expecting more."

"There is more," Harry the gunslinger said with a smile, "but this is enough for our purpose. Welcome to All World, for what it's worth." He walked a short distance from the door, then, sat down on the grass. The women sat around him, six on each side, forming a semi-circle. Harry the wizard looked at his friends, then, walked over and sat down across from his twinner. The others were reluctant at first, then followed suit; Moody found a large, smooth stone to sit on, which was necessary, because one of his legs was fake. Soon the circle was complete.

Sirius spoke first. "Before we get this started, I'd just like to say one thing." Looking at Narcissa he sad "Cissy, I'm glad to hear you're not longer with that pitiful excuse of man. I know you're not the Cissy from my world, but, the way I look at it, you're still family. I only wish my Cissy would smarten up like you and get away from him."

The twinner of his cousin smiled. "Thankee-sai, Sirius. I'm sure she wishes that she could do something, but, if she is anything like me, she might be too frightened to free herself. I only had the courage to annul my marriage after we received news that Malfoy had died in a battle."

"Why get an annulment if you thought you were widowed?" Ginny asked.

"As I said in your world, we were never really a united couple. I may have been foolish enough to believe in blood purity then, but I never approved of. . ." She broke off then, gathering her courage. "I never approved of Voldemort's tactics." The woman looked as though she had just eaten a sour piece of meat. Tonks, the gunslinger closest to her, put an arm around the woman. Cissy smiled at the younger woman before continuing. "I wanted nothing more to do with the Malfoy family and the Death Eaters, so I asked Lord Black to annul my marriage, and, to my eternal gratitude, he did so." Her smile took on a sinister quality. "I also did it to spite the bastard."

"Actually," Gabrielle spoke, "that made Draco the bastard." Everyone was silent for a moment. Then, they all burst out laughing.

"Good for you, Cissy!" Sirius howled. Then, he took on a determined look. "I'll have to offer my Cissy that option someday."

The male gunslinger nodded in acknowledgement of this. "You will have her thanks for that, Sirius, I have no doubt." Then, he addressed the group as a whole. "Now then, our palaver has begun. Question us, and we will answer you."

Clearly then, the wizards realized, it would be up to them to initiate the dialogue. Harry though of a question, but Tonks asked first, a good one as it turned out.

"Why did you bring only these twelve with you? Why not Ron or Neville, or Sirius for that matter? Why them at all?"

The gunslingers all frowned at that. "Don't like that one, eh?" Tonks asked.

"No," the gun toting Harry answered. "It is a hard question."

"But a fair one," the Hermione at his left said. "Although, it had many answers."

"Answer it," Moody insisted.

"Very well, then," Astoria said, defiantly. "Which question do you want answered first?"

"She only asked one question," Ron pointed out.

"She asked four," his sister's twinner corrected. "Why did he not choose you; why did he not choose Neville; why did he not choose Sirius; and why did he choose us specifically? Which one do you want us to answer first?"

"Why you lot?" Tonks answered. "Not that I don't think you did a good job or anything, but why did he choose you all as the only ones?"

"I had no choice," the older Harry answered. "They would not allow me on such a mission alone." He looked at his younger twinner. "And, I have learned that I am weaker when I am alone. Only with them, am I at my strongest."

Pavarti smiled. "In other words, ka-tet." The wizards looked at her in confusion.

"What?" multiple people asked simultaneously.

"Quartet?" Harry asked.

The gunslingers frowned at him. "Ka-tet," the elder Harry stated "isa two syllable word made of two separate words put together. I noticed some differences in our dialects. Do they not know the High Speech in your world?"

Harry looked at Hermione, who shook her head. "I've never heard of a language called the High Speech or which used the words ka and tet."

"And if Hermione doesn't know about it," Ginny said with a smile. "It probably doesn't exist."

"There are a lot of things Hermione doesn't know about that exist," Luna said, smiling. "And a lot of things which she doesn't think exist, but do."

Hermione glared at the other girl. "We can argue philosophy later; what does ka-tet mean?"

The older Luna smiled sweetly at her. "It means 'one from many' or, more literally, "companions of fate." Ka-tet is the place where many lives are joined by fate."

"What does that mean?" Sirius asked.

Katie Bell smirked. "It made little sense to me when I first heard it. That's the poetic version."

"So, a ka-tet is a team then?" Neville asked.

"Actually, it's a type of team," Susan replied. "Daphne, you're good with the High Speech. Could you help?"

Daphne nodded. "A tet is a group of people with similar goals or destinies. The wizards of your world, or at least your nation, for example, could be considered a tet. There are three types of tets. First, there are those which are purely a tet, like , there are An-tets, in which the members share a strong, emotional bond. An-tet can also imply sexual intimacy or even a marriage."

At this, a number of the younger wizards blushed. The gunslingers smiled, both at them and at their own private joke. "And, ka-tet?" Harry asked.

Padma took over from there. "Ka, like many words in the High Speech, has multiple meanings. It can be translated as destiny, duty, life force, or consciousness. In the low speech, it means a place to which one needs to go. Those in a ka-tet are bound together not only by their own friendship, but by ka."

"So, they really are 'companions of fate?'" Tonks the auror mused.

Astoria nodded. "Te-ka, which means 'ka's friend' or 'ka's mate' and refers to the members of a ka-tet, are bound together for their lives. Scholars say that only death or treachery can break the bond."

Cissy spoke up then. "There are philosophers who say that death and treachery are only spokes in the wheel of ka, and so the bond of ka-tet can never be broken."

Susan smiled. "So, mayhap 'soul mates' is a better translation than 'companions of fate.'" The other gunslingers shared a smile with her. It was a secretive smile, for they had kept an important fact from the wizards. They were the rarest variety of tet: a ka-an-tet, a rare form of ka-tet in which all the members were also an an-tet. It was probably better if they did not know that yet, especially the younger ones.

Hermione looked at her double, or rather, her twinner. "You actually believe this?"

"You don't?" the older woman asked casually.

Hermione frowned. Her voice held a defiant tone. "You sound like Luna. I've always believed in free will. I don't believe in fate, and I find it hard to believe that another me would."

"In all honesty," the other replied lightly, "I don't hold much faith in ka as destiny either. I accept duty, life force, and consciousness, but I share your belief in free will." Her tone turned serious. "But ka-tet is not just about 'many lives joined by fate.' It's also those who have shared khef, and I have seen enough to believe in that."

"What's khef?" her younger self asked.

Daphne spoke again. "Like ka, khef has many meanings. The most common are water, birth, and life force. But it also refers to a sharing of minds."

"Sharing of minds?" The younger Hermione looked skeptical.

Gabrielle spoke at this point. "Think of it as a strong form of empathy that the members of a ka-tet share. It happens so naturally that the te-ka often don't realize that it is happening. A ka-tet is most easily noticed by those outside it. Remember that ka also means consciousness."

Hermione was somewhat appeased by this. "That makes sense, I suppose. If you're around someone long enough, it would be natural to gradually become more aware of them than other people are."

Lupin looked thoughtful. "It does make sense. The Marauders, I suppose, were a ka-tet, and I always thought that James, Sirius, and Peter always knew me better than I knew myself."

Sirius smiled at him. "Funny, I always thought the same about you and James, and Peter, I suppose."

"So that's why you chose them," Lupin concluded. "They're you're best allies."

"Smart," Moody grunted reluctantly.

Suddenly, Neville spoke up. "But, what about Ron?" he looked at the famous Gryffindor trio. "Ask anyone at school, if anyone's a ka-tet, it would be you three."

Harry stared at his older twinner. "That's right, why isn't Ron with you? He's my best mate."

The gunslinger looked back at the wizard and shook his head sadly. "No, Harry. Ask yourself this, how was it the Order knew you would be at. . ." he searched his memories of the time he was in the younger Harry's mind for the name, "at the Ministry of Magic tonight. You did not tell anyone save for the five who went with you."

Harry shot a look at the older wizards. Lupin was the one who answered. "Dumbledore contacted us. He said that you were on your way to the Ministry and that we had to go after you." Lupin scowled and looked at Ron. "He never told us how he knew what you were doing, and we were in too big of a hurry to ask."

Harry felt as though he had been kicked in the stomach. He turned to Ron, not knowing that everyone else from his world was doing the same. The redhead looked as though he was about to be sick. "C'mon, Harry. You don't believe him, do you? We're best mates! We-"

Moody pointed his wand at the rambling teen. "Legilimens!" Ron fell back at the mental assault. It lasted only a moment. When it was done, Ron was shivering like a frightened rabbit and Moody was, if possible, angrier.

"The little bastard's been spying on you for Dumbledore for years," he seethed.

Harry was in shock. "No, that's not possible! Ron was my first friend!"

"Funny how that happened, isn't it?" Hermione asked, her voice shaking. "You said you met the Weasleys because Hagrid didn't tell you how to find the platform. I was told where it was by my guide. Odd how you were never told, but the Weasley family just happened to show up when you needed them."

A sick feeling came over all them. "Dumbledore . . ." he whispered, unable to believe what he was being suggested.

"I don't know what your Dumbledore is like," the older Harry snarled, "but ours was a cold, manipulative madman."

"That can't be. . ." Lupin started, but was cut off by the gunslinger Tonks.

"The bastard tried to wipe out most of the world's population to try to bring the survivors under his control and create some kind of golden age."

Padma shook her head. "No golden age could have come of such evil. Great trees do not have rotten roots."

The gunslinger Harry nodded. "Mayhap your Dumbledore is a better man than ours was, but do not try to deny this fact: he is not the saint he appears to be."

The wizard Harry gulped. "So, the Weasleys, Hagrid-"

His twinner cut him off. "Hagrid was a dupe, but he is a noble man. His only fault is that he trusted the old man too much. As for the Weasleys, does one not sit beside me as my te-ka?"

The elder Ginny smiled a sad smile. "It is the fault of many in my family that they trusted Dumbledore too much, but my father is brave and true, and Bill, Charlie, and Fred and George are likewise. They have always been your friends. Percy . . . He did not support Dumbledore, but he was always in the Council's, cry your pardon, the Ministry's pocket."

Ginny looked at her older twinner. "And, my mum?" her voice was filled with fear.

The elder looked sadly at the younger. "I am sorry, but she was even more trusting of the old man than our father. She would do anything for him if he but suggested it. She would not hesitate to try to control us herself."

The younger Ginny broke down in tears. Harry put his arm around her. The other wizards shared a mutual feeling of despair. Then, Lupin looked up.

"I notice you keep speaking of Dumbledore and his supporters in the past tense."

The gunslinger Harry nodded. "Most definitely. He was killed in the last battle of what we now call the Second Great War of Britain. We got rid of some of Riddle's remaining supporters, too. They defected to the old man in exchange for life and power in the new order."

Cissy shook her head. "The ka-mai, cry your pardon, ka's fools, would have been killed, of course. They failed to learn from their previous lord."

Tonks nodded in agreement. "Molly Weasley was sentenced to life in prison for her crimes. Luckily for her, Azkaban is no longer what it was." When the wizards looked at her, she smiled. "We replaced the Dementors with suitable guards: goblins, vampires, trolls, and the like, all talented and deadly, but infinitely more benevolent."

"Numerous supporters of Dumbledore switched to our side at varying times in the war, as they came to understand what the old man was really planning," Susan said, picking the story effortlessly. "They were given leniency. Hagrid and Arthur both proved themselves allies in the final battles."

"What about my brothers?" Ginny asked.

Moody spoke up then. "And what about the rest of us, damn it? Don't tell me I was working for someone like that!"

The elder Harry smiled. "No, you were one of the first people to see the old man as what he was, along with McGonagall and Bill and Fleur Wealsey."

"Fleur Weasley?" Ginny shouted. "You mean that Bill married her?"

Gabrielle frowned at her. "What's so wrong with my sister? She is smart, brave, and attractive; any man would want her. Your brother was lucky to win her."

The elder Ginny nodded. "I admit I was shocked too, at first, but those two belong together, and Bill is very happy with her."

Pavarti smiled at the girl. "You have a number of nieces and nephews thanks to those two. Be grateful."

The younger Ginny did not look grateful, but decided not to force the issue. "What about the others?"

"Charlie," the gunslinger Hermione began, "allied with us soon after Bill did. He was one of our most valuable allies."

"Charlie?" Ginny asked.

Daphne Greengrass grinned wickedly. "He brought some 'friends' with him to aid us."

The other gunslingers sported similar smiles. "Dragons are very good friends to have," Tonks informed the wizards. The foreign mages nodded in understanding. They could all easily imagine the kind of damage a single dragon could do on the battlefield. Multiple dragons would be guarantee victory against a lesser opponent, but not against the likes of Dumbledore.

"The twins," Astoria said, taking up the story, "were able to figure it out on their own. Fred and George were not only formidable warriors, but genius inventors. They were the ones who created the spells on our guns."

The wizards looked at them in surprise. "What spells?" Neville asked.

"Bullet transference spells," answered Padma, despite not carrying a gun or bullets of her own. "Did you not notice that, those of us who used them, did not have to reload our guns during the battle?"

The teens blushed somewhat. They had not noticed. Hermione and Harry were particularly mad at themselves for being so inobservant; they were the ones, after all, who were most familiar with such weapons.

Katie began examining the cartridges in her belt. "See this?" she asked, holding up a bullet. She slipped it back into her belt and stood up. The woman drew her gun and aimed at the river. "You owe me a bullet," she said, before firing a single shot. The dry crack was not the deafening roar that had been heard in the Ministry, but it was still loud. She sat back down and pulled out the same shell casing she had earlier, but now the bullet was gone.

"The charm's designed so that the shells replace themselves as they're fired. You're still limited to however many shells your belts can hold, and it only works for the belts keyed to that particular gun. That means I can't use Harry's gun and get any more than a half dozen rounds out of it if I'm not wearing his gun belts, too, but it's better than having to reload every six shots."

"Why even use the guns at all?" Hermione asked. "Wizards in our world are forbidden to use firearms."

Susan nodded at her. "For years, it was the same in this world. Mages held such weaponry in contempt and so refused and outlawed them. That was a great advantage our side held in the war."

"I understand," Hermione said. "Pulling a trigger is quicker than casting a spell." After a moment she added, "You were all much faster, especially you. . ." She struggled with the last word for a few seconds. "Harry," she finally said.

The elder Potter bowed his head to her. "Thankee-sai, Hermione."

"I suppose that means, 'thank you,'" Sirius said wryly.

Harry nodded. "Sai means 'sir' or 'madam' in the High Speech. Usually, it is used to honor an older man or woman, but it can be used to show respect for a youthful individual."

Hermione blushed a little. The Harry from her world supposed that she felt how he did when people stared at his scar.

Cissy began speaking then. "Percy, as your twinner said, was a staunch supporter of the failed, old government, right until the end. He was too ambitious to switch sides at first, then too proud, but he was given a limited sentence. His crimes were considered lesser than Molly's. He was released this past year," she told the Weasley daughter.

"Ka-mai," the witch said. The others all looked at her for a moment . . . and then burst out laughing.

"Yes," her twinner replied. "He was a ka-mai, indeed. My father remained loyal to Dumbledore the longest, but the old man couldn't hide the truth from him forever. He joined our side near the defeat of Voldemort."

"I think I'm starting to realize how complicated this must have been for you," Lupin said. "You had to fight the Death Eaters, the Order of the Phoenix, and the 'Council.'" He shook his head. "I'm impressed. I don't think I could have managed such a campaign. But, tell me, what about my own twinner?"

Astoria smiled. "Harry and Moody were able to convince him and Kingsley to join around the same time as Arthur Weasley. Harry, Hermione, and Charlie were able to gain his trust and he allied himself to us."

Sirius looked annoyed. "Hey, what about me? Forget about your own godfather, Harry?"

His words had a greater effect than Sirius could have ever anticipated. The gunslinger Harry hung his head. The All World incarnations of Hermione, Ginny, Tonks, and Cissy all looked devastated. None of the others were smiling anymore.

"Oh no," Sirius moaned. "Let me guess, I'm dead, aren't I?"

All World Harry nodded grimly. "Bellatrix Lestrange killed you in the. . . Department of Mysteries is your name for it."

The younger Harry was horrified. "You mean that Sirius actually was killed because of my stupid rescue attempt?"

His twinner agreed with him, but Hermione of All World vehemently denied it. "No, Harry! Sirius died because Bellatrix Lestrange. She was the one who cast that curse. You tried to save him. You must never think otherwise."

"But Sirius would never have even been there if I hadn't gone to save him!"

"And you only went to save him because Voldemort tricked you!" the woman yelled. "If blame rests with anyone other than Lestrange, it's Voldemort!"

Harry started to protest once more, but Sirius walked behind him and put a hand on his shoulder. "I can't speak for the me of this World, Harry," he said, kindly. "But, if he's anything like me, he would have been proud to die for you." Harry looked deep into the man's eyes, then buried his face in Sirius' chest, crying.

The others were silent for a moment, letting the young man deal with his emotions. When he finally stopped, it was Gabrielle who spoke first.

"Don't be grieved, Harry. The Sirius of your world is very much alive."

Harry looked at her with a tear-stained faced and smiled. "You're right." Then, he turned to his twinner. "Thankee-sai, you saved my godfather."

The older Harry smiled gently. "Listen to your Hermione more often," he said, noting how the pair twitched just a little at his choice of words. "She is wise and knows you well." The others looked at the Harrys.

The younger one spoke first. "Hermione says I have a saving-people-thing."

"Then you are Harry Potter!" Pavarti cried, and the sound of laughter filled the land, as the others joined in with her.

Soon, everyone was catching their breathe. Astoria was the first to sober up. "You must understand," she said slowly and seriously. "We were in a war, and now you are too. People die in wars. To hope for more is to ask for a miracle."

Harry understood what was implied. "Who else?" he asked.

Susan spoke softly. "Thousands died, but, in terms of people you knew . . . all too many."

Harry felt as though a lead ball had been dropped in his stomach.

Luna spoke. "In addition to Sirius, Lupin, Dobby, Hegwig, and Fred all found the clearings at the ends of their paths in the war against Voldemort." Poetic, but it did little to improve the overall mood of the group.

Harry's mind was reeling. Lupin, Fred, Dobby, Hegwig . . . Too many of his friends had been killed by the Death Eaters, or Dmbledore's forces or whoever. He knew all too well what it must have been like for Tonks and Ginny to hear about this. She had lost one of her brothers in a manner that could never be undone.

Padma began speaking then. "It could have been worse. Our Tonks was nearly killed, too, and Hagrid was captured and tortured. Neville was almost burned to death, but he is all right now."

The Hermione of his world was the first to notice Luna's choice of words. "You said this was during the fight with Voldemort. What about Dumbledore and the Council?"

All World's Tonks shook her head. "No one against the Council. Cissy was right; it was weak. Fell without a fight, it did. The old man, however, was a tougher foe than Voldemort."

Katie grunted. "Kingsly went down fighting. Arthur lost an eye, and Charlie lost an arm, but they're both alive and well. Buckbeak the hippogriff and a number of threstrals were killed. A number of our allies that you haven't met yet died, too. One of them was Horace Slugworth, a retired potion's master, one of Dumbledore's most trusting friends. Hagrid was killed, near the end-"

"NO!" Harry yelled, unable to suppress his grief.

"Sadly, it's true," whispered his gunslinger twinner. "Snape was killed a while after Hagrid."

"Snape?" Sirius asked.

Cissy nodded. "He was perhaps Dumbledore's greatest victim. The old fiend was the only one who knew of a certain crime in Serverus' past. It was a crime and a mistake, the one crime as a Death Eater that he hated himself for. Dumbledore knew how desperately Serverus wanted to undo this mistake, and he used that desperation to make Serverus his puppet. He almost got the man killed by Voldemort, but he survived and switched to our side."

Harry looked at his other self angrily. "And you let him join you?"

The other man shrugged. "We needed all the help we could get. I didn't trust him, but I knew that he could be helpful. This was before, of course, I knew of his past. He never told me until shortly before his death. I think he knew the end was coming. I would have trusted him from the beginning if he had told me, but he was at loathe to do so. Mayhap it was his pride, or, mayhap, he didn't want to be forgiven."

"You, forgive Snape? Are you sure you're Harry Potter?" the young Ginny asked.

"Must have been some mistake," Tonks said, thoughtfully.

Padma nodded. "It was perhaps the saddest mistake a man can make."

"Well, spill the beans already!" Sirius said, excitedly.

"Beans, what beans?" Gabrielle asked.

"Share the dirt on that greasy git. Tell me what he did that was so bad."

"He inadvertently caused the Voldemort to kill the one true friend he ever had," All World's Harry answered. "She was the only woman he ever loved."

The smile instantly dropped from Sirius' face. The younger Harry could understand it. He didn't see how he could ever forgive Snape, but he actually pitied the man. In a way, it explained why Snape was always so unpleasant.

"Damn," Sirius muttered. "Never thought I'd ever feel sorry for Snape."

"Strange, isn't it?" the older Harry whispered. "I never would have thought it either. Yet, now I do."

"So," Moody said, breaking the dour atmosphere. "You went through the door to change our timeline so that things would work out better for us, right?"

"Wrong," All World's Harry answered. "I did not know where or when the door led. My only clue was the words written on it, THE BOY WHO LIVED. Considering we both share that title, it was not much to go on. It was only by ka that I came to you when I did."

"What?" the witch Hermione yelled. "You went through a, a world-crossing door with no idea what was on the other side of it?" Sirius, Lupin, and Ginny did not look pleased.

"Not very smart, Harry," the younger Tonks noted with a laugh.

"But you had a reason, didn't you?" Luna asked. "You went through the door because you had to, not because you wanted to."

The gunslinger in question smiled at her. "It is as you say, Luna-sai. I had no choice. An enemy had travelled from my world to your own, and I needed to stop him."

"Who was this enemy?" Tonks asked, feeling her blood start pumping. She was an auror, a witch who hunted down magical criminals. When you got down to it, the hunt was one of the most fundamental aspects of life. Simple, the way Tonks preferred things.

"A man who should have stayed dead," Cissy spat. "Lucius Malfoy.
